subject: Anthropology
title: Social Stratification
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Social Stratification.

hook.text: One grave holds gold jewelry and fine pottery. The next holds nothing but bones. Inequality is written in burials.

buildup.text: Social stratification is the division of society into ranked layers based on wealth, power, and prestige.

discovery.text: Archaeology detects stratification through burial goods, house sizes, diet differences, and access to luxury items.

twist.text: Stratification wasn't inevitable. It emerged with surplus. When there's more than enough, someone always takes more.

climax.text: Material inequality leaves material traces. Archaeology reads social hierarchy directly from the ground.

punchline.text: Graves reveal who had power and who didn't.

quiz.question: How do archaeologists detect social stratification?
options: Through burial goods, house sizes, and diet differences
By asking descendants of ancient people
